comment.body:
i tell you one thing.
Nobody became rich on daytrading. 
Many ppl are trying to hit the jackpot, but in order to do that, you 1) either have to oversize yourself and hope to be lucky, or 2) you need extremely high probability setup (like the April sell-off), to take a big size on the bet.
In reality, the intraday risk is very high.

Only the patient long-term players stay in the game long and build a wealth.
The rest is lie.
daytrading was only an extra for me, but I only traded when I see a very good setup, when it is clear, and I'm sure in the move.
otherwise it is just blind-shooting.
Avoid that.
or not... your money.

For now, I don't short the market, I just hedge my exposures and let my gold, silver, oil etc. investments to print along with gov bond yields (i will start to scale in again).
And waiting for the opportunity to buy some dip for the next years again after April scale in.
Daytrading is only to make some small extra growth.

Never use the same accoutn for investment and intraday trading + I never use more than 10% of my wealth for this. But never the whole 10%, only fractions by trades.
